primary_concern: Sepsis with multi-organ dysfunction and acute kidney injury
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL), thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL), anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL), and bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggest systemic inflammation consistent with sepsis. Acute kidney injury (AKI Stage 1 to Stage 3) is documented with creatinine progression from 2.1 to 8.9 mg/dL (excluded outlier), though the most recent 2.1 mg/dL remains concerning. Platelets have remained below 100 K/uL for 48 hours, meeting SOFA coagulation criteria.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2 points
Creatinine rise of 2.1 mg/dL meets KDIGO Stage 1 criteria; excluded outlier suggests progression to Stage 3
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5× baseline

prioritized_risks.guideline_source: Laboratory Reference Ranges and Clinical Thresholds
prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL for ≥48 hours meets SOFA coag score criteria and indicates severe coagulopathy
Lactate >2 mmol/L meets part of septic shock criteria, but vasopressor requirements and fluid resuscitation status unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection-focused workup
Begin fluid resuscitation and monitor for vasopressor requirements
Check INR/PTT and fibrinogen to evaluate coagulopathy severity
doctor_handoff: Review for evidence of infection source, assess volume status, and confirm AKI etiology. Monitor platelet trend and consider transfusion threshold discussion.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) and thrombocytopenia (95 K/uL) documented
Lactate elevates to 4.2 mmol/L with persistent thrombocytopenia (88 K/uL)
Platelets remain low at 92 K/uL; excluded AKI Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) suggests acute decompensation

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
